summary: The hexagonal split and Components V2 renderer are solid, but there are blocking Discord capture/present edge-case bugs that can break or degrade the RLHF signal.

issues.description: Capture runs with an empty bot token when `botToken` is omitted, which sends `Authorization: Bot ` and causes invalid REST reads instead of failing fast.
issues.explanation: Failing fast on missing credentials prevents ambiguous capture behavior and avoids recording incomplete/invalid feedback caused by unauthorized API calls.

issues.description: Webhook URL is concatenated with a hardcoded `?wait=true&with_components=true`, which breaks URLs that already include query parameters (e.g., `thread_id`).
issues.explanation: Using `URL`/`searchParams` preserves existing webhook query params and guarantees valid URL composition for all Discord webhook targets.

issues.description: Reaction 429 handling recurses without an attempt bound, which can loop indefinitely under persistent rate limiting.
issues.explanation: An iterative bounded retry keeps behavior deterministic and prevents runaway recursion/hangs while still respecting Discord rate-limit recovery.

blocking_issues.issue: Missing `botToken` is converted to an empty token for capture reads.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Feedback capture can proceed with invalid auth semantics and produce unreliable RLHF data.
blocking_issues.fix: Require `botToken` at capture time and throw a clear error if absent.

blocking_issues.issue: Query string is appended unsafely with string concatenation.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Webhook posts can fail in valid deployments that rely on existing webhook query params.
blocking_issues.fix: Build the request URL with `new URL()` and `searchParams.set()`.
